overview: From 2026-06-02 to 2026-06-03, elizaos/eliza had 40 new PRs (16 merged), 5 new issues, and 12 active contributors.

topIssues.title: orchestrator: sub-agent completion narration drops/garbles the deliverable (narration-envelope)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

When the planner narrates a coding sub-agent's `task_complete` back to the user, the **deliverable** (the printed value / command output) is frequently dropped, truncated, or buried under process chatter â€” while the underlying sub-agent work is correct. This is a *presentation* defect, not a capability one.

Evidence: a live 48-prompt sweep (30-scenario battery + smart/normie/harden suites) across all three backends found **0 capability-broken defects** â€” every answer was correct, every spawn produced its artifact, every safety probe handled. The **only** remaining defect class is this narration-envelope.

## Manifestations (all observed live)

1. **Deliverable drop on combo / printed-value tasks.** On the no-change-set completion path, `composeNarration` â†’ `stripToolTranscript(response)` (`sub-agent-router.ts`) deletes the entire `[tool output: â€¦] â€¦ [/tool output]` block. For a "print a value / run a command and report the output" task (no files changed â†’ no diff), that block *is* the deliverable, so it's gone before the user sees it.
2. **Value truncation by the parent model.** The synthetic `task_complete` memory is re-rendered by the parent `RESPONSE_HANDLER` (a weak planner in some deployments), which truncates the value â€” e.g. `2026-06-02` â†’ `202`, a BTC price `70234` â†’ `705`. Per-backend signature: opencode truncates, codex emits verbose process narration + `Completed <task>` banners, claude is cleanest.
3. **Stale retry attempts leak.** When a first attempt errors (e.g. `session_state_lost` / an ACP crash) and a respawn recovers, the user sees *both* `"the sub-agent was blocked / crashed, let me try again"` **and** the eventual deliverable.
4. **Process chatter.** Sub-agents (esp. codex) narrate their steps (`"I'll load the workspace context first"`, `"rg is not installed"`, `"reverse.py already exists"`); the parent relays it.

## Proposed structural fixes

**(a) Carry the printed-value deliverable verbatim (fixes #1, mitigates #2).** A structural carrier that mirrors the existing verified-URL carrier:
- *Producer* (`sub-agent-router.ts`): on the `task_complete && !changeSet && verifiedUrls.length === 0` path, recover the captured stdout from the **raw** `response` (before `composeNarration` strips it) via the inverse of `stripToolTranscript`, and stamp it as `metadata.subAgentDeliverable` (gated to a short single block, â‰¤2KB, so multi-KB transcripts stay on the model-rendered path). *(I have a ready, tested producer patch for this.)*
- *Consumer* (`evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts`): when `subAgentDeliverable` is present, relay it **verbatim** instead of letting the model re-summarize/truncate it â€” parallel to how `subAgentVerifiedUrls` is already consumed.

**(b) Terminal-reply filter (fixes #3, #4).** Emit only the *terminal* sub-agent reply; suppress stale `blocked`/`failed`/`state_lost` attempts that a later retry superseded, plus raw tool chatter and `Completed <task>` banners. **Caveat â€” needs care:** the `session_state_lost` error event is currently *coupled to the respawn trigger* (re-injecting it is what prompts the parent to respawn), so suppressing the user-facing narration requires an internal-only routing path that keeps the respawn signal. This is the part that needs design discussion.

**(c) Already shipped â€” source-side mitigation.** #8158 adds an output-discipline section to the scaffolded sub-agent operating manual ("lead with the deliverable, don't narrate process, no `Completed <task>` banner, a bare workspace has no SOUL.md/USER.md â€” don't hunt for them"), which measurably reduced #4 at the source (codex re-probes went from a verbose banner to a bare `5050` / `2026-06-02`).

## Coordination note

`sub-agent-router.ts` and `evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts` were recently rewritten on `develop`; this should be coordinated with that work rather than landed blind. Happy to open the (a)-producer + (a)-consumer as a focused PR if the approach looks right.

topIssues.title: desktop-ffi: prewarm violates GGML_ASSERT(n_tokens_all <= cparams.n_batch) on eliza-1-0_8b after shim loads
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## context

after #7988 lands the windows-x86_64 ffi build path, `libeliza-llama-shim.dll` loads into `bun:ffi` with all 39 symbols resolved. the very next layer â€” prewarm â€” crashes with a ggml batch-sizing assert on `eliza-1-0_8b` text bundle.

## crash

```
GGML_ASSERT(n_tokens_all <= cparams.n_batch) failed
```

repro: `~/.eliza` already has the `eliza-1-0_8b-128k.gguf` bundle staged; on first activation the prewarm batch builder asks the runtime to decode more tokens than the context's `n_batch` slot allows. fatal.

## environment

- platform: windows-x86_64
- backend: cuda (cuda 12.4, msvc, ninja, llama.cpp pinned to the ref in `build-llama-cpp-desktop-dylib.mjs`)
- shim: built from `eliza_llama_shim.c` with the stub set added in #7988
- bundle: `bundles/0_8b/text/eliza-1-0_8b-128k.gguf` from default-user `~/.eliza/local-inference/`
- runtime: `desktop-llama-adapter.ts` â†’ `eliza_llama_decode_unified` path

## what i think is going on

the prewarm path appears to construct a batch that targets the manifest's declared context window (128k tokens) without first clamping against the actual `cparams.n_batch` value the ffi context was created with. on linux/macos this might be masked by larger default `n_batch` or different prewarm sizing; on windows-cuda the assert fires.

candidates (i don't want to put words in anyone's mouth):

1. prewarm should size the batch off `cparams.n_batch`, not `n_ctx`.
2. context creation on the desktop adapter should set `n_batch >= prewarm_tokens` for the chosen bundle.
3. the bundle manifest's recommended prewarm token count should be exposed and used as both inputs.

## scope

- not blocked by anything outstanding once #7988 lands.
- separate from the build-time fixes in #7988 (which are infrastructure) â€” this is a runtime sizing bug one layer down.
- related context PR: #7983 (local-inference dflash discovery + runtime router wiring).

topIssues.title: USE_SKILL output wrapping leaks raw bash invocation JSON to user reply
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

When a sub-agent invokes a skill via USE_SKILL (e.g. weather skill running \`wttr.in\`), the bot's reply to Discord contains the raw bash command + JSON wrapping AS prose, then the clean output, glued together:

\`\`\`
{"cmd":["bash","-lc","curl -s \"wttr.in/Paris?format=%l:+%c+%t+%h+%w\""]}{"output":"Paris: đźŚ¦ď¸Ź +15Â°C 68% â†™3km/h"}Paris: đźŚ¦ď¸Ź +15Â°C 68% â†™3 km/h
\`\`\`

Three layers leaked verbatim:
1. \`{"cmd":["bash","-lc",...]}\` â€” the bash invocation envelope
2. \`{"output":"..."}\` â€” the captured-output envelope
3. The actual user-friendly result (also rendered, fortunately)

## Repro

Live 2026-05-25 07:49 UTC. Probe: \`@bot use the weather skill for paris\`. Trajectory \`tj-1be9e601583b7e.json\`. Stage 0: \`simple=true reqTool=false candidates=["WEATHER_FETCH"]\` reply="On it." (routing-layer promoted to planning via the candidateActions->planning fix). Iter 1: \`USE_SKILL slug=weather mode=auto\`. The skill captured stdout via a wrapper that emits the cmd+output envelopes; the planner's REPLY text concatenated all three.

Probe \`use the weather skill to get weather for tokyo\` had the same shape but the wrapping was less visible because the final paraphrase was cleaner.

## Why it matters

User-facing UX: the bot dumps internal shell invocation details to Discord. Looks broken; reveals "I am running bash". Pollutes the channel.

## Suggested fix

USE_SKILL action's result construction should produce a clean \`userFacingText\` (the actual command output) separate from the diagnostic \`text\` (which can include cmd + envelopes for trajectory recording). The planner-loop's \`preferredFinalMessageFromToolOrModel\` already prefers \`userFacingText\` when set with \`verifiedUserFacing: true\` â€” USE_SKILL should opt in for skills whose stdout is the user answer (weather, github, healthcheck, etc.).

Or simpler interim fix: in the skill-invocation wrapper, when producing the REPLY text, parse + strip the \`{"cmd":...}\` and \`{"output":...}\` envelopes before they're concatenated into the model's REPLY context.

## Acceptance

- \`use the weather skill for paris\` returns just \`Paris: đźŚ¦ď¸Ź +15Â°C 68% â†™3 km/h\`, no JSON envelopes.
- No regression on skills that already produce clean output (some did in this same sweep â€” github, healthcheck honest refusals didn't leak).

Reported via 50-probe deep sweep on milady deployment 2026-05-25.

topIssues.title: orchestrator: served-200 build false-flagged as stale by mtime freshness gate (GAP-C retry/recall regression)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
### Summary

`verifyLocalTarget` in `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts` false-positives on a healthy, served static app, marking it "dead" purely because its local file mtime is older than `session.createdAt - 5s`.

### Root cause

In the completion-URL verification pass (`annotateUnverifiedUrls`), after a URL probes a healthy HTTP 200 (`probe()` returns `status === null`), the code still consults `verifyMappedLocalUrl` â†’ `verifyLocalTarget`, which applies a wall-clock freshness gate:

```ts
if (requireFresh && stat.mtimeMs < sessionStartedAtMs - 5000) {
  return `mapped local target was not updated during this session: ...`;
}
```

A static app (e.g. `color-pop/index.html`) that exists, is non-empty, and serves HTTP 200 gets flagged "dead" because the deploy step copied the build into place and preserved the source file's mtime (mtime skew vs `session.createdAt`).

### Impact (GAP-C chain)

1. The false-dead triggers a spurious verify-retry.
2. The retry suppresses the round-1 `task_complete`.
3. The in-flight retry session trips `newerTaskSince` in `coding-session-changes.ts`.
4. The REAL diff is withheld â€” so a follow-up "what did you change?" wrongly answers "no captured file diff".

### Fix

The live HTTP 200 probe is authoritative for a served URL. The mtime-freshness comparison must not override a healthy 200; reserve the freshness gate for cases where the URL is NOT serving (where the local file is the only liveness signal). The missing-file and empty-file checks remain unconditional.

Tracked by PR with a regression test that pins both the served-200 (not flagged) and unserved (still flagged) cases.

topIssues.title: inline WEB_FETCH planner routing (#8097) regresses live-info on non-Anthropic runtimes
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

PR #8097 added two things:
1. A **keyless `WEB_FETCH` action** (`packages/agent/...`) so non-Anthropic models can answer live info inline. **This is fine â€” keep it.**
2. **Inline WEB_FETCH planner routing** in `packages/core/src/services/message.ts`. **This regresses live-info on non-Anthropic runtimes and should be reverted.**

## The regression

On a gpt-oss / Cerebras (non-Anthropic) runtime, the inline routing added to `message.ts` breaks live-info queries (BTC price, weather, etc.):

- It **perturbs the working opencode sub-agent webfetch path** that previously answered these queries reliably.
- `WEB_FETCH` **never reliably surfaces** through the planner action surface on these runtimes, so the planner is left mis-routing â€” live-info queries stop returning real values.

Live-testing confirmed that reverting `message.ts` to its pre-#8097 state (`git checkout <pre-8097> -- packages/core/src/services/message.ts`) plus a stronger spawn-task prompt restores correct behavior: BTC/weather queries again return real values via the opencode webfetch tool.

## The inline-routing additions to revert (all in `message.ts`)

- the live-info **force-include** block in `collectV5PlannerCandidateActions` that surfaces `WEB_FETCH` past the context gate;
- the **`webLookupBackstop`** branch in `buildV5PlannerActionSurface` (+ the `V5PlannerActionSurfaceSummary.webLookupBackstop` field);
- the `WEB_LOOKUP_ACTION_NAMES` / `WEB_LOOKUP_DIRECT_ACTIONS` constants, `findCanonicalWebLookupActionName`, and `findPreferredWebLookupActionName`;
- the `"web"` branch added to `shouldPreferDirectCurrentCandidateActions`;
- the web/coding reordering + `findPreferredWebLookupActionName` swap in `inferAckIntentCandidateActions` / `inferDirectCurrentRequestCandidateActions` / `findDirectOwnedActionSuggestion`.

## What stays

The keyless **`WEB_FETCH` action** in `packages/agent/...` is harmless and stays â€” capable models can still use it. Only the `message.ts` planner *routing* is reverted, so upstream matches the working live state and future syncs don't re-break live-info.

PR follows.

topPRs.title: feat(plugin-task-coordinator): odysseus-style orchestrator UI
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Adds a full **chat-shell UI for the multi-agent orchestrator**, modeled on the
[odysseus](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus) frontend (MIT) and
rebuilt from scratch in **React on `@elizaos/ui`**, wired to the **existing
elizaOS runtime**. This is **frontend only** â€” there is no new backend; all
task/session state continues to live in `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.

The new surface renders at **`/odysseus`** (and the existing **`/orchestrator`**
route) as a full-window app: a labeled session sidebar, a rich composer, a theme
engine, the memory/skills/notes/settings panels, and the full set of tool views.

> Credit: the original UI/UX design is from **odysseus** (MIT). This is an
> independent React reimplementation on elizaOS's own stack â€” none of the
> odysseus Python backend is used.

## What's included

**New `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/odysseus/` shell (~40 files):**
- `OdysseusShell` root + `SessionSidebar` (folders, multi-select, pin, drag,
  resize) + collapsible 48px `IconRail` (mutually exclusive with the sidebar)
- `Composer` with slash-command + emoji autocomplete and a bottom action row
- Theme engine (`ThemeMenu` / `PresetsPanel`): preset recolor + customize
  (font / density / background), mapped onto elizaOS semantic CSS vars
- `MemoryPanel` (memories/skills/add/settings), `SkillsPanel`, `NotesPanel`,
  `SettingsPanel`
- Tool views: tasks, models, email, calendar, gallery (+ editor), compare,
  research, voice, group chat, cookbook, document library, admin
- A cross-view **window manager** (`WindowManager` + `useWindowControls`):
  drag / resize / edge-snap / minimize-to-dock (`MinimizedDock`)
- `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useEscapeClose`, `useTaskRoom`, `useChatSubmit`

**Orchestrator render helpers:**
- `orchestrator-markdown` (via `marked`), `orchestrator-plan`,
  `orchestrator-reasoning`
- `orchestrator-stream` now always renders the user's own `stdin` messages
  (preserves the existing `senderKind === "user"` fix)

**App-shell `fullBleed` capability (`packages/ui`):**
- Opt-in `fullBleed?: boolean` on `AppShellPageRegistration`
  (backward-compatible). When set, the host shell mounts the page edge-to-edge
  via `ViewRouter` â€” **no header, no tab bar, no routed padding** â€” so a view
  that owns its whole window has no surrounding dashboard chrome.
- This **replaces the prior hardcoded `tab !== "orchestrator"` header
  special-case** in `RoutedShellContent` with a general, declarative flag.
- `/orchestrator` and the new `/odysseus` route are registered `fullBleed: true`.

**Other:**
- adds the `marked` dependency to the plugin
- isolates each `*.live.e2e.test.ts` suite's default chromium profile dir so
  parallel vitest file execution can't collide on the ProcessSingleton lock

## Verification

Built on a fresh branch off current `develop` and verified there:

- biome check â€” clean across all 53 changed source files
- `tsgo --noEmit` (plugin) â€” **0 errors** against current `develop` APIs
- `tsgo --noEmit` (`@elizaos/ui`) â€” 0 errors in `App.tsx` / `app-shell-registry.ts`
- plugin unit tests â€” **19/19** pass
- plugin build â€” `tsup` + views bundle + type declarations all succeed
- live e2e (`*.live.e2e.test.ts`) â€” **26/26** pass against a running dev stack,
  and Playwright render checks confirm the host header is absent on `/odysseus`
  (full-bleed) and the global chat overlay behaves correctly

## Scope / notes

- **Frontend only.** No actions/providers/services added; state stays in
  `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.
- The `fullBleed` flag is opt-in and changes no existing page's behavior.
- Some per-view actions render honest empty/disabled states where elizaOS has no
  corresponding client method yet (e.g. email IMAP/SMTP, calendar CalDAV,
  gallery image backend, voice TTS/STT) â€” these light up unchanged once the
  matching runtime routes exist. Happy to file follow-up issues for these.

Screenshots / a short screen recording can be added on request.

## Related

- Closes #8188 (proposal/motivation).
- Unblocked by #8187 â€” a develop-side `biome` format fix for `plugin-app-control/src/views/ViewManagerView.tsx` that currently red-fails the repo-wide **Format Check** gate (and every open PR). This PR's Format Check goes green once #8187 lands.

## CI status (for reviewers)

This PR's own gates are green: Format Check (this PR's files), Server Tests, unit-tests, lint-and-types, integration-tests, coverage on changed files, gitleaks, check-pr-title, route-coverage, iOS Simulator Build, Homepage Build, Electrobun Desktop Contract, Cloud Live E2E.

The currently-red lanes are **pre-existing on `develop`**, not introduced here â€” proven by #8187 (a 1-file format-only change) failing the *identical* set:

- Plugin Tests â€” `@elizaos/plugin-rlm` (`server.test.ts` spawns `/nonexistent/python`)
- Client Tests â€” `FineTuningTuiView.test.tsx` (plugin-training)
- Zero-Key Deterministic E2E, `bun run dev onboarding chat`, `env:audit:check`
- Android Debug Build â€” `shortcuts.xml` deep-link audit (`run-mobile-build.mjs`)
- Format Check (biome) â€” develop's `ViewManagerView.tsx` (â†’ #8187)

A short screen recording / screenshots of the live `/odysseus` surfaces can be added on request.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): add Odysseus workbench and operator inspector
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Current Status

This PR is intentionally **draft** and should not merge as one unit. It is the large Conductor/Odysseus operator-inspector stack and currently carries too much unrelated history for review or shipment.

Latest large-branch head: `0892cb7dc504c89c65d0c10f7ba25a5491812689` on `codex/conductor-inspector`.

Recommended ship path is the split PR stack:

- #8183 `chore: sync bun lock workspace entries` â€” lockfile-only base hygiene; fixes current workspace lock drift on `develop`
- #8184 `fix(app): restore dev smoke chat mount` â€” app/Vite dev-smoke fix stacked on #8183
- #8186 `fix(ci): align zero-key app smoke with startup shell` â€” CI-smoke drift cleanup stacked on #8184
- #8181 `test(orchestrator): cover ACP plan events and word-split text` â€” stacked on #8186
- #8182 `fix(orchestrator): guard stream identity and task deletion` â€” stacked on #8186
- Later: operator inspector drawer and Odysseus view/component port as separate reviewable PRs

## What This Stack Contains

- Odysseus-style orchestrator workbench surfaces and `/odysseus` routing from the local `nubs/orchestrator-view-feature` stack
- Conductor-inspired operator detail drawer concepts for sessions/timeline blocks with Input, Output, Events, and Usage tabs
- Stream identity hardening so rendered blocks remain traceable to message/event/session records
- Non-chat/full-bleed routing fixes so orchestrator/Odysseus controls are not covered by global chat chrome
- UI safety fixes including destructive-action confirmation and markdown URL sanitization
- Dev-only Vite/Bun CommonJS interop shims so local app dev smoke can mount the real chat UI again without changing production resolution
- API CORS support for `ELIZA_UI_PORT` so non-default Vite UI ports are accepted by the local API server

## Current Split-Stack Validation

- #8184: manual Dev Smoke passed, manual Cloud Tests passed, focused app/CORS/local smoke validations passed
- #8186: local startup/runtime tests, plugin-browser/elizaos tests, full assistant-home browser spec, reset-to-onboarding browser spec, ChatComposer unit coverage, scenario-runner PR coverage, Biome, and `git diff --check` passed
- #8186 fresh manual runs on head `e78a1b7f5e109771e41485df13d62879ad32d29c` are in progress:
  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839814087
  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839815886
  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839817661
  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839819151
- #8181 and #8182 are based on #8186 and are clean under `git diff --check origin/codex/ci-smoke-drift-cleanup...HEAD`; their focused tests passed before the final #8186 base-head advance and their diffs are unchanged

## Local Validation On The Large Branch

- `bunx biome check packages/app/src/shims/*.ts packages/app/vite.config.ts packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.ts packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false --skipLibCheck --ignoreConfig packages/app/src/shims/*.ts`
- `bunx vitest run packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.test.ts` passed: 7 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test -- test/route-coverage.test.ts test/ui-smoke-coverage.test.ts test/view-interaction-coverage.test.ts` passed: 19 tests
- Isolated UI probe confirmed `/chat` mounts, no page errors, and `chat-composer-textarea` is visible

## Notes

The Conductor study conclusion still stands: borrow Conductor's operator experience, not its deterministic DAG execution model. For Eliza, the immediate value is rendering and recovering from the DTOs the orchestrator already streams; a DAG editor, polling-worker queue model, retry matrix, and DLQ/compensation machinery should stay out unless product evidence says otherwise.

topPRs.title: feat(ui): continuous-chat overlay + restored app shell, and a Storybook catalog
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
> **Draft-quality note for reviewers:** part of this PR (the `App.tsx`/`Header.tsx` restoration) reverts the intentional pill-only cleanup on `develop`. That is a maintainer call by design â€” flagged explicitly, not slipped in. The branch is **synced up to `develop` and `MERGEABLE`** (see "Sync status").

## What this is

Two separable bodies of work on `packages/ui`:

### 1. Her-style continuous-chat overlay + restored full app shell  *(behavioral â€” reverts part of the pill-only strip)*
- `ContinuousChatOverlay` â€” a self-contained, presentational glass "continuous chat" overlay (whisper lines that dissolve over any view, expand-to-thread, voice, ChatGPT-style micâ†”send swap, full a11y, safe-area + mobile-nav-offset aware). Controller injected via prop (`useShellController`); no bespoke state duplication.
- Restored the full **3-panel chat workspace** (`ConversationsSidebar | ChatView | TasksEventsPanel`) and the **header navigation**, both unwired by the June pill-only cleanup (the components/nav model were never deleted, just disconnected).
- The overlay is the single shared chat input: it floats over every view, and on the chat tab `ChatView` hides its in-view composer (`hideComposer`) so there's no duplicate composer â€” both read the same conversation.
- `MINIMAL_SHELL` flag (`shell-chrome.ts`) gates a minimal conversational-OS home vs. the full workspace.

### 2. Storybook catalog for `@elizaos/ui`  *(purely additive)*
- Real `@storybook/react-vite` catalog (`packages/ui/.storybook`), **424 stories** across primitives, composites, shell, and presentational feature surfaces.
- Harness: Tailwind v4 plugin, `@elizaos/*` source aliases mirroring `vitest.config.ts`, a `mockApp()` decorator (under `src/` so it shares the preview module graph), and a `node:fs` stub.
- A documented gap (`.storybook/README.md` #3, tracked in #8177): i18n/model-hub stories that transitively import `@elizaos/core` can't render yet (Vite dep-optimizer resolves core via its Node entry â†’ `plugin-manager` â†’ `fs-extra`). Those stories are held back rather than shipped broken.

## Verification (this branch, merged up to develop)
- `packages/ui` unit tests: **479 pass** (overlay/shell-chrome/cloud-shell suites incl. behavioral window-shell + mic/send-guard cases).
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui lint` (biome) and `typecheck`: **clean, 0 errors**.
- Storybook catalog render-verified headless (Playwright) across all surfaces.
- A multi-dimension review (eliza-conventions / hacky / correctness / react-quality / fits-architecture / test-quality) ran with adversarial verification; all confirmed findings were fixed (notably two mobile-layout bugs: a phantom bottom-nav padding gap under `MINIMAL_SHELL`, and the overlay composer occluding the mobile bottom nav).

## Sync status
**Merged up to `develop`; `MERGEABLE`.** The only conflict was `App.tsx` (resolved toward the restoration). Drift handled: dropped an orphaned import + stale comment; removed `voice/VoiceWaveform.stories.tsx` because `develop` deleted that component in a perf pass ("kill VoiceWaveform"); reconciled `bun.lock` for develop's new workspace packages.

## CI triage â€” the red checks are NOT introduced by this branch
This branch changes **only `packages/ui` + `bun.lock`** (0 server/plugin source files). Green on every lane this code affects: **Client Tests, unit-tests, lint-and-types, integration-tests, coverage, Format(biome) for ui, Electrobun, CodeQL**. The reds are inherited from `develop` (currently red there, maintainers actively repairing) or CI infra:
- `Format Check (biome)` â†’ `@elizaos/plugin-app-control` (untouched here; develop's own unformatted file).
- `Server Tests` / `Plugin Tests` â†’ server/plugin packages (untouched here; red on develop).
- `Dev Smoke (onboarding chat)` / `Zero-Key Deterministic E2E` â†’ red on develop (in-flight "fix(ci)" commits).
- `env:audit:check` â†’ `@discordjs/opus` prebuilt-binary 404 (CI/upstream native-build flake; dep byte-identical to develop).
- `All Tests Passed` â†’ aggregate gate that fails because of the above.

**One interaction for a maintainer eye:** `dev-smoke/bun-dev-onboarding-chat.spec.ts:198` asserts `getByTestId("chat-composer-textarea")` visible at `/chat`. This branch hides the in-view composer there (overlay is the input). If the restored input model is accepted, that testid/assertion needs reconciling (e.g. the overlay composer exposing the canonical testid). Not edited here â€” those specs are under active repair on `develop` and this touches the restore-vs-pill direction.

## For reviewers
If the shell restoration needs more discussion, the **Storybook catalog (#2) is additive and can be split into its own PR** off `develop` â€” happy to do that on request. Tracked follow-up: #8177.

topPRs.title: fix(app): restore dev smoke chat mount
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Stack

This PR is stacked on #8183 (`chore: sync bun lock workspace entries`) because current `develop` needs the lockfile-only env-audit fix first.

## Summary

Restores the app dev-smoke chat mount on Bun/Vite canary by adding dev-only CommonJS interop shims for packages that Vite currently fails to resolve correctly during `bun run dev`.

Also lets the API CORS helper accept `ELIZA_UI_PORT`, which is needed when the Vite UI uses a non-default smoke-test port.

## Why

The lockfile-only PR #8183 still fails Dev Smoke on current `develop` with:

- `getByTestId('chat-composer-textarea')` not found on `/chat`

This PR fixes that failure. Manual Dev Smoke on this head passed: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26829349502

The Vite aliases are scoped with `apply: "serve"`, so production builds keep resolving the real packages.

## Validation

- `/home/nubs/Git/iqlabs/eliza-labs/eliza-acp-plan-event-tests/.tmp/bun-install-canary/bin/bun install --no-save`
- `/home/nubs/Git/iqlabs/eliza-labs/eliza-acp-plan-event-tests/.tmp/bun-install-canary/bin/bun x biome check packages/app/src/shims/*.ts packages/app/vite.config.ts packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.ts packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.test.ts`
- `/home/nubs/Git/iqlabs/eliza-labs/eliza-acp-plan-event-tests/.tmp/bun-install-canary/bin/bun x tsc --noEmit --pretty false --skipLibCheck --ignoreConfig packages/app/src/shims/*.ts`
- `/home/nubs/Git/iqlabs/eliza-labs/eliza-acp-plan-event-tests/.tmp/bun-install-canary/bin/bun x vitest run packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.test.ts` passed: 7 tests
- `/home/nubs/Git/iqlabs/eliza-labs/eliza-acp-plan-event-tests/.tmp/bun-install-canary/bin/bun run --cwd packages/app test -- test/route-coverage.test.ts test/ui-smoke-coverage.test.ts test/view-interaction-coverage.test.ts` passed: 19 tests
- `git diff --check origin/codex/bun-lock-workspace-sync...HEAD`
- Manual `Dev Smoke` workflow passed on head `41fe0e55964cdc94e4cd3cc5ad3f99554432dba9`: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26829349502

topPRs.title: fix(ci): align zero-key app smoke with startup shell
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- carry the upstream stale-test alignment for the current startup shell and registry/browser-route tests
- hydrate full `/api/status` once when launch/boot progress reports ready, so ChatView receives the runtime model before unlocking the composer
- align the assistant-home ui-smoke flow with the current chat workspace, typed send, scripted browser STT, push-to-talk, and desktop pill suppression
- keep the inline chat composer in its push-to-talk release state after the transcript fills the draft, so release can still submit the held turn
- update reset smoke coverage to assert the current bootstrap-token first-run form after Reset Everything
- align the scenario-runner PR coverage contract with the current assistant-home spec

## Root Cause
The stacked app-smoke branch fixed the dev chat mount, but broader Zero-Key/Scenario runs still hit stale assertions and then exposed a startup-state gap: ready launch/boot progress mapped `model` to `undefined`, which ChatView interpreted as no configured provider. The assistant-home spec also expected old home-only selectors and embedded-pill behavior that no longer exists in the current chat shell. While replacing those stale selectors, the full browser smoke exposed a real push-to-talk regression: the inline composer swapped from the release mic to the send button once STT text filled the draft, so pointer release could no longer submit the held voice turn.

## Validation
- `bun x biome check packages/ui/src/state/startup-phase-runtime.ts packages/ui/src/state/startup-phase-runtime.test.ts packages/app/test/ui-smoke/assistant-home-flow.spec.ts packages/app/test/ui-smoke/helpers.ts packages/app/test/ui-smoke/reset-returns-to-onboarding.spec.ts packages/elizaos/src/commands/plugins.test.ts plugins/plugin-browser/src/routes/__tests__/browser-bridge-companion-revoke.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test src/state/startup-phase-runtime.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/elizaos test src/commands/plugins.test.ts`
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-browser test src/routes/__tests__/browser-bridge-companion-revoke.test.ts`
- `node packages/app-core/scripts/ensure-shared-i18n-data.mjs`
- `bunx playwright install chromium`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e test/ui-smoke/assistant-home-flow.spec.ts --project=chromium -g "captures first-run, assistant home, chat suppression, and view pill states"`
- `bun run --cwd packages/scenario-runner test -- src/executor.test.ts src/runtime-factory.test.ts src/scenario-pr-workflow.test.ts src/__tests__/deterministic-action-coverage.test.ts` passed: 43 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test src/components/composites/chat/chat-composer.test.tsx` passed: 3 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/scenario-runner test -- src/scenario-pr-workflow.test.ts` passed: 10 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e test/ui-smoke/assistant-home-flow.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed: 4 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e test/ui-smoke/reset-returns-to-onboarding.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed: 2 tests
- `bun x biome check packages/ui/src/components/composites/chat/chat-composer.tsx packages/ui/src/components/composites/chat/chat-composer.test.tsx packages/app/test/ui-smoke/assistant-home-flow.spec.ts packages/app/test/ui-smoke/reset-returns-to-onboarding.spec.ts packages/scenario-runner/src/scenario-pr-workflow.test.ts` passed with one existing warning in an unrelated literal-string assertion
- `git diff --check`

## Manual Workflow Dispatches
Fresh runs on head `e78a1b7f5e109771e41485df13d62879ad32d29c`:
- Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839814087
- Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839815886
- Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839817661
- Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839819151

## Follow-up Fix (`c2a4cf8a29`)
- restore keyless UI-smoke stubs for terminal, screenshare, and task-coordinator contracts so Zero-Key can exercise real route/interaction behavior instead of generic placeholder views
- lift the embedded shell pill above the active view surface and align orchestrator visual coverage with the current visible assistant pill
- move the app-control view manager back onto theme tokens so plugin tests no longer fail on hardcoded dark/cyan styles
- tighten the screenshare smoke locator to the `Host` heading now that the view also has a `Start host session` button

### Additional Validation
- `bun x biome check packages/app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-smoke-api-stub.mjs packages/app/test/ui-smoke/plugin-view-cases.ts packages/app/test/ui-smoke/screenshare-gui-interactions.spec.ts packages/ui/src/App.tsx plugins/plugin-app-control/src/views/ViewManagerView.tsx`
- `node --check packages/app-core/scripts/playwright-ui-smoke-api-stub.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control test src/views/ViewManagerView.test.ts` passed: 2 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/ui test src/components/shell/__tests__/HomePill.test.tsx src/components/shell/__tests__/shell-assistant-flow.test.tsx` passed: 16 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test:e2e test/ui-smoke/screenshare-gui-interactions.spec.ts --project=chromium` passed against a verified stub stack
- combined zero-key subset passed: 60 tests across plugin visual coverage, orchestrator GUI, screenshare GUI, task-coordinator GUI, game-operator GUI, and terminal command contract

### Remote Rerun Status
- New head: `c2a4cf8a290e9c722d0aebb8439002e25fa89c0f`
- Previous Cloud Tests manual run finished success: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839819151
- Fresh manual reruns on this head:\n  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26843352716\n  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26843352839\n  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26843352758\n  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26843352732

## Follow-up Fix (`0526cd122d`)
- add the missing `@elizaos/logger` source alias to the Electrobun Vitest config, matching the existing source aliases for `@elizaos/core` and related workspace packages

### Additional Validation
- `bun x biome check packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun/vitest.electrobun.config.ts`
- `bun run --cwd packages/app-core/platforms/electrobun test src/native/desktop-window.test.ts src/rpc-handlers.test.ts src/dynamic-view-rpc-schema.test.ts src/surface-windows.test.ts src/dynamic-views/host.test.ts` passed: 47 tests
- `git diff --check`

### Remote Rerun Status
- New head: `0526cd122ddfb7f5cfde83428ddfbb65b12088ea`
- The previous manual Tests run failed only in `Electrobun window and dynamic-view coverage`; its earlier 60-test keyless UI-smoke subset passed remotely.
- Fresh manual reruns on this head:\n  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844050307\n  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844050270\n  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844050326\n  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844050230

## Follow-up Fix (`30b24bf97e`)
- add `@elizaos/logger` source aliases to plugin Vitest configs that already alias `@elizaos/core` to source, preventing unbuilt workspace package resolution failures in app-control and related plugin suites

### Additional Validation
- `bun x biome check plugins/plugin-app-control/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-elizacloud/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-gitpathologist/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-mcp/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-local-inference/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-shell/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-scape/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-wallet/vitest.config.ts`

- `git diff --check`

### Remote Rerun Status
- New head: `30b24bf97e5e6d435335f4f8cb0b43e275610d2`
- The previous manual Tests run passed Server, Client, Electrobun, live gates, and keyless UI-smoke, then failed only at app-control verification on the same `@elizaos/logger` source-resolution issue.
- Fresh manual reruns on this head:\n  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844986834\n  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844986920\n  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844987111\n  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26844987332

## Follow-up Fix (`89e400c7e7`)
- add `@elizaos/logger` source aliases to computeruse, coding-tools, and local-inference audit Vitest configs that point `@elizaos/core` at source

### Additional Validation
- `bun x biome check plugins/plugin-computeruse/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-coding-tools/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-local-inference/vitest.audit.config.ts plugins/plugin-app-control/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-elizacloud/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-gitpathologist/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-mcp/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-local-inference/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-shell/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-scape/vitest.config.ts plugins/plugin-wallet/vitest.config.ts`
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-computeruse test -- test/helpers/screenshot-quality.test.ts src/__tests__/browser-auto-open.test.ts` passed: 6 tests
- `bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control test -- src/services/__tests__/app-verification.integration.test.ts src/services/__tests__/verification-room-bridge.test.ts src/actions/views-management.test.ts` passed: 19 tests
- `git diff --check`

### Remote Rerun Status
- New head: `89e400c7e73ff6e8a57ac450fc38570c3e6e845c`
- The previous manual Tests run passed app-control verification and then failed only at computeruse browser auto-open on the same `@elizaos/logger` source-resolution issue.
- Fresh manual reruns on this head:\n  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26845741148\n  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26845741211\n  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26845741175\n  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26845741183

## Follow-up Fix (`f607dab12b`)
- add the missing React runtime dependency for `@elizaos/app-model-tester`, matching its direct React imports and fixing fresh plugin-test installs
- build `@elizaos/plugin-x/lifeops-message-adapter` as an explicit package entry so LifeOps scenario resolution can import the subpath that `plugin-x` already advertises

### Remote Rerun Status

- The previous manual Tests run passed the earlier logger-alias slices and then failed only in Plugin Tests (`@elizaos/app-model-tester` missing `react`) and Zero-Key scenario resolution (`@elizaos/plugin-x/lifeops-message-adapter` missing built subpath).
- Fresh manual reruns on this head:\n  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26847189406\n  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26847189362\n  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26847189372\n  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26847189377

completedItems.title: fix(cloud): guarantee response-token budget for all reasoning models

Eliza Cloud inference returns `content: null` (and **bills the consumed tokens**) for reasoning models when `max_tokens` is too small to cover both the hidden chain-of-thought and an answer.

Confirmed live against `api.elizaclo

The per-agent web UI is the natural way users interact with a provisioned sandbox. It was disabled by default in `managed-eliza-config` because the original concern was that a public web UI exposed without auth would leak agent stat

- the "first poster" slot is **read** (`completionHasPostedFromOtherSession`) before the awaited delivery loop (`a

A weak planner (gpt-oss-class) sometimes hallucinates its own `TASKS`/spawn-action argument object into `messageToUser`, so the user receives the raw envelope:

```json
{"task":"Fetch the current Bitcoin price","agentType":"opencode","appro
